task_completion.description: The agent accessed the Aerobridge admin interface and logged in.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Django admin dashboard is visible at http://localhost:8000/admin/ with 'Site administration' and 'Welcome, admin' (or similar logged-in indicator) shown.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the 'Add flight plan' page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form to add a new flight plan is visible in the Django admin interface.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the correct name for the flight plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Name' input field in the flight plan form contains the text 'Mumbai Coastal Survey'.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the correct Plan file JSON.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Plan file json' text area in the flight plan form contains exactly `{"name": "Mumbai Coastal Survey"}`.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the correct Geo JSON.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Geo json' text area in the flight plan form contains exactly `{"type": "Point", "coordinates": [72.8777, 19.0760]}`.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the flight plan and remained logged in.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (usually a green banner) indicating the flight plan 'Mumbai Coastal Survey' was added successfully is visible, and the admin interface shows the user is still logged in.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the Aerobridge admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser (like Chromium or Firefox) navigating the application, rather than terminal commands (like curl or python scripts) making HTTP requests.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Django admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface looks like the standard Django admin panel, not a mocked HTML page or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not modify the database directly via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of sqlite3 or other database CLI tools being used in the terminal to insert the flight plan record directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a flight plan in the Aerobridge Django admin interface at http://localhost:8000/admin/. The expected flight plan name is 'Mumbai Coastal Survey'. The Plan file json must be exactly {"name": "Mumbai Coastal Survey"} and the Geo json must be exactly {"type": "Point", "coordinates": [72.8777, 19.0760]}. The agent must log in with username 'admin' and password 'adminpass123'.
